leadText: Headlong Rush (Japanese: ぶちかまし Headlong Rush) is a damage-dealing Ground-type move introduced during Generation VIII, in Pokémon Legends: Arceus.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation VIII 1.2 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.3 Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Headlong Rush inflicts damage and then lowers the user's defensive stats for three turns. Pokémon Legends: Arceus Category Physical PP 5 Agile Strong Power 100 80 120 Accuracy 100% 100% 100% Action speed modifier Self 0 0 +5 Target 0 +3 0 Additional effect Turns 3 3 3 ? ? ? ? Lowers the user's defensive stats Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Headlong Rush inflicts damage and then lowers the user's Defense stat and Special Defense stat by one stage each.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description LA The user smashes into the target in a full-body tackle. This also lowers the user’s defensive stats. S V The user smashes into the target in a full-body tackle. This also lowers the user’s Defense and Sp. Def stats.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Headlong Rush is tied with Precipice Blades for the highest base power of all Ground-type moves, excluding Z-Moves , Max Moves , and G-Max Moves . In Japanese, ぶちかまし Buchikamashi (Headlong Rush) is a sumo term which refers to the frontal collision of the two sumo wrestlers right at the beginning of a match. The inspiration for the move's Ground type is likely a play with the words 武 bu (bravery, courage), ち chi (ground), ちか chika (underground) and 噛まし kamashi (blow, hit). This is also the likely reason why this move is considered a punching move , and why it can be learned by Hariyama . In Super Smash Bros. Ultimate , Charizard 's forward smash is also known as Headlong Rush, though Charizard cannot learn the move in core games. This game was released prior to Headlong Rush's introduction as a move in the core series .
